greeting: You've found the ruins, then. Most turn back when the thorns close in. Sit a moment — I can tell you what these stones remember.
Careful where you step. The flagstones here are older than any living king, and what lies beneath them is older still.
Another seeker on the King's Road. I keep the camp fire burning for folk like you.
quest: These ruins were a scriptorium once, before the thorns took them. The monks who worked here copied texts about the Grail — maps, hymns, prophecies. Most were lost, but fragments remain in the lower chambers. I can show you the way in, if you've nerve enough.

I've spent twenty years cataloguing what the ruins surrender. Each season the earth shifts and reveals something new. Last autumn I found a map fragment that matches none of the known roads. It points beyond Ravensgate, toward the highlands.
idle: The thorns grow thicker each year. Sometimes I think the ruins want to be forgotten.
I found a carved lintel yesterday with script I cannot read. Something about a covenant.
The camp is modest, but the fire is warm and the tea is strong. Help yourself.
